label: Narrow-shell 1BR / 1 bath, SIDE-CORRIDOR SHOTGUN — a single hallway runs the full depth of the house along one side; great_room, bedroom, bath, and kitchen bud off it one after another from front (street) to rear, in that order. No room is ever reached through another room.

adjacencies.note: hall's front end opens into great_room — no door, codebase rule: hallway↔public is always open-plan. This is also where the main entry sits.
great's rear wall = bedroom's front wall — geometry-only, NO door. Bedroom's actual access is the hall; this keeps the building a clean rectangle at the seam.
bedroom's only door — opens onto the corridor.
bedroom and bath sit next in the sequence — shared wall only, no door. Acts as the acoustic buffer between the bedroom and the wet rooms at the rear, same convention as the 2BR narrow catalog.
bath's only door — opens onto the corridor. Reachable by the occupant or a guest without walking through the bedroom.
shared plumbing wall — bath and kitchen cluster at the rear end of the house, same wet-stack convention used throughout the catalog.
corridor's rear end opens into kitchen — no door. Kitchen is the terminal room of the shotgun sequence.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_nw_side_corridor_bath_hall_1br
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: Narrow lots spend their entire buildable width on ONE room column plus the corridor, so the corridor is pinned tight (0.95-1.1 m, just above the PH 0.9 m minimum) to leave as much of the remaining ~4-4.5 m for the room column itself. With only 4 rooms in the sequence (vs. 5 for the 2BR version) the same corridor length now serves a shorter house, so the compact threshold drops to 40 sqm buildable.

notes: Narrow-shell catalog topology — first 1BR entry for the 'narrow' target_shell
(frontage ~5-7 m, depth ~12-16 m). Adapts the 2BR narrow 'side_corridor_bath_hall'
idiom by dropping the standard bedroom; the sequence shortens from 5 rooms to 4,
shrinking the corridor's total area cost proportionally.
Floor band: roughly 20-32 sqm buildable — the smallest footprint in the 1BR
catalog, fitting the tightest narrow-lot subdivisions common in dense PH infill.
Layout intent (canonical, corridor on the right):
[GREAT (entry) | hall] — [BEDROOM | hall] — [BATH | hall] — [KITCHEN | hall]
This reuses every door/hall idiom already validated in the 2BR narrow catalog —
bedroom_door, bath_door, hallway-always-open — just with one fewer room in the
sequence.
Trade-off: the corridor still runs the ENTIRE depth of the house, so on the
narrowest lots it remains a meaningful share of the buildable width even with
fewer rooms. A future variant could shrink the hall to just the bedroom+bath pair
and let great_room/kitchen open-plan directly to each other, cutting the corridor
length roughly in half — not yet drafted.
No carport declared — NCP form. Narrow lots rarely have side-setback room for a
carport; a front-loaded covered parking bay can be added by trimming great_room's
front depth and adding a building_void.
Side setbacks: assumes at least one side approaches a firewall/party-wall
condition (PD 1096) with no window openings on that face — the corridor side is a
reasonable place to put that wall, since a corridor doesn't need windows the way a
bedroom does.
Fallback: none declared yet — first draft, not yet validated against a
smaller-lot fallback.
BUGFIX (2026-07-16): left_anchored originally listed all four of great/bedroom/
bath/kitchen, forcing every one of them flush to the left wall. Since hall (the
only room to their right) has a single left edge, forcing multiple stacked rooms
to ALSO touch hall's shared wall pins them all to the SAME right edge too --
i.e. identical widths for rooms with very different area/dimension needs -- and
this, combined with the solver's hard rule that every 'great_room'/'master_
bedroom' habitable room must touch an exterior wall (window requirement),
over-constrained the model so no layout existed at ANY lot size (confirmed via
CP-SAT infeasibility across a full sweep, 4-120 sqm floor). Only 'bedroom' is
actually sandwiched in the stack with no natural front/rear wall access (great
gets the front wall, kitchen is hard-pinned to the rear wall already) -- it's
the only room that NEEDS the left-wall touch for its window. Fixed by dropping
great/bath/kitchen from left_anchored, keeping only 'bedroom'. Solves COMPLIANT,
0 warnings, from ~8x14 m lots up; see lot_size_sweep_1br.py.
